channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Indian parliament's Lower House passes controversial bill that would change Muslim endowments
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — The Lower House of India's parliament early Thursday passed a controversial bill moved by Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s Hindu nationalist government to amend laws governing Muslim land endowments.
item.dc:creator: AIJAZ HUSSAIN and SHEIKH SAALIQ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Alleged MS-13 gang leader linked to nearly a dozen murders, on the run for years nabbed in Long Island: DOJ
item.description: An MS-13 gang leader was arrested in Long Island on Tuesday for his alleged role in nearly a dozen murders in California and Nevada, the Justice Department announced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Violent storms cut through the South and Midwest, spawning tornadoes and killing 1 person
item.description: Violent storms cut through a wide swath of the South and Midwest, spawning tornadoes and killing at least one person, knocking down power lines and trees and ripping roofs off homes.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu is rocked by a new scandal linking his close advisers to Qatar
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s office is once again ensnared in scandal after police arrested two of his close associates this week on suspicion of accepting money from Qatar to promote a positive image of the Gulf Arab state in Israel.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ariana Grande, Cynthia Erivo tease new 'Wicked: For Good' footage during CinemaCon victory lap
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — When Ariana Grande,Cynthia Erivo and director Jon M. Chu took the stage in Las Vegas last April to tease “Wicked” months before its theatrical release, there was a cautious optimism that the long-anticipated film would be a hit.
item.dc:creator: KRYSTA FAURIA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Outrage grows over Maryland man's mistaken deportation to El Salvador prison
item.description: In the 22 days since Maryland resident Kilmar Abrego Garcia was mistakenly deported to a notoriously violent prison in El Salvador, his young son has sought comfort in the scent of his missing father's clothes.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Democratic states sue to stop billions in public health funding cuts
item.description: HHS said the funds, totaling $11.4 billion, were primarily used for COVID-19 response including testing, vaccination and hiring community health workers.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Charges dismissed against Illinois man accused of assault by Rep. Nancy Mace
item.description: U.S. Rep. Nancy Mace claimed the assault came in response to her efforts to bar transgender women from using women's restrooms on federal property.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Brams, NewsNation

item.title: New bill seeks to alleviate burden on Florida citrus growers as orange groves disappear
item.description: As Florida citrus growers continue to face hardships, causing some to make the difficult decision to sell their orange groves, a new piece of bipartisan legislation is aiming to alleviate some of the burden.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Montana's anti-transgender bathroom restrictions are on hold under a judge's order
item.description: HELENA, Mont. (AP) — A judge on Wednesday temporarily blocked a Montana law that restricts transgender people's use of bathrooms in public buildings. The measure, which Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Police say the 2023 Nashville school shooter hid mental health issues from doctors and family
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The shooter behind the 2023 Nashville elementary school attack that killed six people, including three children, had been obsessively planning it for years while hiding mental health issues from family and doctors, a police report released Wednesday reveals.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, KIMBERLEE KRUESI and TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Air traffic controllers to get more support after a fight and latest near miss at Washington airport
item.description: Air traffic controllers at Washington's Ronald Reagan National Airport will be offered crisis counseling and additional supervision after a fight in the tower and another alarming near miss two months after a midair collision between a passenger jet and an Army helicopter killed 67 people.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: District attorney lashes out at ICE for detaining suspect in Boston during a trial
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A district attorney in Boston harshly criticized the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement on Wednesday after one of its agents detained a suspect while he was on trial. ICE agent Brian Sullivan took Wilson Martell-Lebron, 49, into custody last week as he was leaving court.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Amazon's last-minute bid for TikTok comes as a US ban on the platform is set to take effect Saturday
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Amazon has put in a bid to purchase TikTok, a Trump administration official said Wednesday, in an eleventh-hour pitch as a U.S. ban on the platform is set to go into effect Saturday.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man high on mushrooms ran red light, caused fatal crash, jury finds
item.description: At one point, when he was asked if he needed medical attention, Frazer responded, "I just want the love.
item.dc:creator: Danny Peterson, NewsNation

item.title: Musk could be headed for a Washington exit after turbulent times at DOGE
item.description: President Trump has said that DOGE will end at some point and Musk will return to running his companies.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US immigration will only recognize 2 genders going forward, ends Biden-era practice
item.description: U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services said it's updating its policies to clarify that it only recognizes two biological sexes, male and female.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Final report details investigation into deadly 2023 Covenant School shooting
item.description: The report touches on the shooter's planning as well as the details unveiled during the investigation.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Number of Tesla attacks soars past 50 as violence targeting Musk's company escalates
item.description: Over 50 Tesla attacks in the U.S. linked to Elon Musk's political views. Incidents range from vandalism to arson, with Pamela Bondi treating them as domestic terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump tariff plan could cost households thousands: New analysis
item.description: A new analysis from The Budget Lab at Yale shows how much a broad tariff plan could cost U.S. households.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Potential Trump tariff plan could cost households thousands: new analysis
item.description: A new analysis from The Budget Lab at Yale shows how much a broad tariff plan could cost U.S. households.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wild weather expected Wednesday gets a rare 'high-risk' designation
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — U.S. government forecasters are using a relatively rare “high-risk” designation — the highest category they use — to warn that a major tornado outbreak appears likely Wednesday in an area that's home to about 2.5 million people.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New documents describe physical condition of man allegedly held captive for decades in Connecticut
item.description: An investigator said the man's skin appeared "to be graying," and that his "physical condition resembles one who would be at a point at the end of their life.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge holds ICE agent in contempt after he detained suspect during a trial
item.description: ICE agent Brian Sullivan detained Wilson Martell-Lebron last week as he was leaving court. But a Boston Municipal Court judge issued a ruling Monday against Sullivan, arguing that he had deprived Martell-Lebron of his rights.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, WGN 9

item.title: Democrats' win in Wisconsin court race a big loss for Elon Musk
item.description: Musk and his affiliated groups sunk at least $21 million into the normally low-profile race and paid three individual voters $1 million each for signing a petition in an effort to goose turnout in the pivotal battleground state contest.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US judge orders Trump administration to restore legal aid to unaccompanied migrant children
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge in California on Tuesday ordered the Trump administration to temporarily restore legal aid to tens of thousands of migrant children who are in the United States without a parent or guardian.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amish buggies targeted in Pennsylvania robbery, attempted robbery
item.description: Some among Pennsylvania's Amish community are concerned that someone may be targeting them, after two buggies were held up at gunpoint, according to State Police.
item.dc:creator: Stan Boney, Fox 5

item.title: Dressing sold at Trader Joe's recalled over undeclared allergens
item.description: A California company has issued a voluntary recall for a dressing distributed to 18 states and sold under the Trader Joe's private label.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, KTLA 5

item.title: Most immigrants at risk of deportation from US are Christian, report finds
item.description: As many as four in five immigrants at risk of deportation from the United States are Christian, according to a new report that calls on their fellow believers to consider the impact of the Trump administration's aggressive deportation policies.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: An ‘administrative error’ sent a Maryland man to an El Salvador prison, ICE says
item.description: President Donald Trump’ s administration has acknowledged mistakenly deporting a Maryland man with protected legal status to a notorious El Salvador prison last month, but is arguing against returning him to the United States because of his alleged gang ties and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Social Security Administration probes origin of website portal outage
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Social Security Administration said Tuesday that it is investigating the root cause of website outages that have affected the “my Social Security” portal where recipients access their benefits.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Watch: Potential albino pygmy killer whale spotted off Hawaii
item.description: Whale watchers around Hawaii are crossing their fingers for another glimpse of what could be an albino whale.
item.dc:creator: Alina Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Aggressive sea lion attacks 15-year-old girl in Southern California waters
item.description: A 15-year-old in Long Beach, California, is recovering after a terrifying ocean encounter with a sea lion that attacked her over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: Evacuations ordered as winds pose challenges for firefighters battling California blaze
item.description: BISHOP, Calif. (AP) — Firefighters stopped the forward spread of a wildfire tearing through a remote area of eastern California, but evacuation orders for residents of hundreds of homes remained in place, officials said Monday evening.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Girl, 9, dies after procedure at California dental office
item.description: A report from the San Diego County Medical Examiner's Office claims the girl was discharged from the facility and remained asleep on the ride home.
item.dc:creator: Delaney White, KTLA 5

item.title: NASA astronauts Butch Wilmore, Suni Williams discuss extended ISS stay
item.description: NASA astronauts Butch Wilmore blasted into orbit in June 2024, as part of a test flight of Boeing’s new Starliner crew capsule. They were scheduled to return about eight days later, but problems plagued the mission.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Who is Trevor Milton, the startup founder and donor pardoned by Trump
item.description: Milton said Thursday in a post on social platform X that he spoke to Trump over the phone and that he signed his pardon of “innocence.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9
